+## Segment Analysis (Info Level 1, ±65% noise)
+- **S1** (Price-Sensitive): q_min=0.081 (actual ~0.05-0.13), WTP=$26, usage=91 units/day, market=272K
+  - Converting at 4% — Plan A T2 quality 0.156 is near the boundary for most S1 customers

+  - After R&D quality ~0.236: still < q_min 0.37 → don't serve until more R&D
+- **S3** (Power Users): q_min=0.145 (NOISY — actual ~0.19-0.24 based on 0% conversion at q=0.187)
+  - 54 leads ALL LOST at quality 0.187 → actual q_min > 0.187 (likely ~0.20-0.24)

+- **E1** (Cost-Cutting): q_min=0.736, WTP=$20/seat, 25-740 seats

+  - High value! Keep targeted dev $100/day
 
-## Current Configuration

+## Priority Actions by Day

+## Key Insights / Lessons
+1. Lead conversion happens ON ARRIVAL — permanently lost if quality < q_min
+2. S3 true q_min is ~0.20-0.24 (NOT 0.145 as noisy estimate suggests) based on 0% conversion at 0.187
+3. S3 is a reputation super-spreader critical for enterprise growth
+4. Plan A T2 keeps S1 profitable (compute < $12 revenue at ~91 units/day usage)
+5. Plan A T3 would lose money: 91 × $0.006 × 30 = $16.38/mo > $12 revenue
+6. S1 subscribers complaining about quota → raised Plan A quota to 300 (happy customers = better retention)
+7. set_targeted_ad_spend is FULL REPLACE (not additive!) 
+8. Social media post addressing quota complaints → builds S1 reputation
+9. Enterprise (E1 DM post about 600-unit quota) → shows interest but quality not yet sufficient
+10. R&D T1 mean=12 days, T2 mean=17 days (both high variance)
